author: @EricFranklin-d2v
text:
I worked on some ideas I had a couple years ago, for a couple different projects. It involved an idea I had for a rolling friction type drive that at its core was very similar to this but not this thought through. Zero backlash and smooth rolling drive was the goal, with the same idea, for two very different applications. I even looked into the eccentric hypocycloid at the time, but it didn't suit the need because I wanted a potential overload slip in the drive for very high spike loads. I wish I had seen this back then.  
There are lots of uses for this concept. One use could be as a rope drive system in robotics with no creep rope for long reaches away from the drive centerline, one could be in a strict scara arm system with a long reach to attain zero backlash, lots more uses here. 
The very valuable lessons learned here: 
1. Dont try to reinvent the wheel but first try and improve on something that may already exist. 
2. Check Robert's channel first 
3. Never stop learning(Im in my 50's:-) 
4. (Dont post unrelated links here:-) 
Great work Robert. We need you to never stop teaching us.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am fascinated by what you were working on mate and i agree there are lots of uses - cheers

replies.author: @EricFranklin-d2v
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  this may could even be used as a pretty solid tank track style drive wheel, Certainly as a no ground contact drive wheel position where it is high and the internal bearing are protected from debris entering. Should be an easy shroud addition to protect it. Or as a precision conveyor belt speed reduction that can handle high load due to making that drive wider. Or as a closed end windlass anchor style winch. Or even just a fixed rope/wire winch. High shear load would be possible, the parts just need to be made to handle the needed shear. The caveat is that  the radius of that drive is larger than a spool with nothing inside it, increasing the force to pull, but with very high gear reduction as you said, that could fit many applications, agreed. And the speed reduction occurs inside the working drive wheel, so no added gear reducer slapped on the side of something and sticking out 😎

replies.author: @EricFranklin-d2v
replies.text: Thinking, what if that step up were actually an inverted step, making the rise of the step negative. I'll definitely play with this thing soon😎

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: I am doing PCB etching right now using acetic acid and peroxide solution (that and a pinch of sodium chloride) so I will hopefully soon have a big batch of copper acetate, and I am sure you know where I am going with that ;) ....... SO my question is have you done any more with your copper nanoparticle research??

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i do mate lol - and no i didn't do much else beside what I showed

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: for some reason this looks like a really good way for someone to make an antibacklash component for things like 3D printers and CNC milling machines that can be made by anyone with simple home shop tools and supplies, (since usually springs in spur gears that if you push against it harder than the spring tension will cause the backlash between mechanical gears to show up, while here if you were to stretch those flexible bands in opposing directions giving more sets of them for more tension while retaining free flow rotation in either direction, you could get rid of that backlash where the gearing system going to instantly change direction on both sides of it and allow up or down ratioing the rotation for faster or slower outputs, (like say someone working on a machine that can deposit exact location of individual atoms for the edges of large depositions ;) )  I really can not wait to be able to test out the full potential of my open case vacuum generation system so I can test out making even magnets using crushed and powdered unwanted shape and size magnets for the raw material that have their magnetic fields embedded into each layer of the prints :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i like that thinking mate - cheers

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering not sure if I have mentioned it but I have reduced iron oxide and leaving most of it as pure iron deposited so the process will definitely be capable of taking powdered iron ore and convert it to the metal directly with out even needingg a mold to pour anything into. (also converted titanium dioxide powder into rutile crystals of TiO2 so there is enough energy to break it apart, and my oxygen removal process and vacuum generation/maintaining system should allow printing pure titanium parts from the oxide as well, just have to finish up that process, (also found a way to temporarily turn bismuth powder in water into a ferro magnetic reactionary substance and once the power is off it goes right back to being diamagnetic so planning on trying it on gold since there is tons of it in water most places, LOL.)

author: @ArchimedesDrive-IMSystems
text:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts about the Archimedes Drive, Robert! It was an absolute pleasure working together to achieve this result: A 3D printable model of the Archimedes Drive publicly available for all tech enthusiasts. We absolutely love your channel and it is amazing how well you explain the working principles of the Archimedes Drive. Keep up the good work:) Cheers!

^Casper

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Oh wow - thanks for taking the time to say that mate - it has been a lot of fun and thanks again to you and the guys at IMS

author: @TylerWatson2

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I guess I have a strange idea of fun - but my guess - so do you lol

replies.author: @TylerWatson2
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering  there's just something magical about making something in CAD and then 3D printing it, and then watching it move in the wind. I keep coming back to my wind turbine to improve the design, and try to get enough power out of it to charge a 12V battery. Been playing with my own version of your stacking planetary gears from a while ago, figuring out how to fix it to my own 3d printed generator. 

I know by now I've spent way more time (and probably money) on it than the value of the energy I'm ever likely to capture out of it, but man it's so fun to just tinker and try things 😊

author: @RichardElniski
text:
The Driven rollers could have a silicone sleeve of varying thickness (0.5 to 10mm) on them. This would
enhance the coefficient of friction with the Sun roller to the orbiting rollers. The Silicone sleeve could
also be used on the Sun roller for better torque and power transfer.  This is an Excellent Model Robert!!

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you Robert, you continue to inspire us!! 
I'm a bit busy in the garden at the moment,
 if you can come up with Anything to prevent this years "Slug-Fest",,,(Organically!!)
~ The Nobel Peace Prize will most certainly be yours!!         ":0)Z!! 
Andrea and Critters.    ....XxX...

replies.author: @VeniceInventors
replies.text: Not speaking from experience but for the sake of the slugs... My suggestion would be to provide them with something far more appetizing to them than what you're growing for yourself, that way they'll spare your crop.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@VeniceInventors Ahh, yes,,,
~ the Sacraficial Slug-Crops!! 
Great idea, but just attracts more slugs, and then they breed happily, until they over-run the garden, eating everything in their path!! Lol!!

Fennel tea, cooled,sprinkled about the garden, and crushed fennel seeds seem to deter the Hordes of leaf munching monsters, as does a thin smear of  Vick's Rub, around my containers, 
so far, this year anyway!! (WOOOHOO!!)

Thank you very much for your kind reply, 
I really appreciate your suggestion, 
 I think perhaps "Slug-Fest!" must have been bothering me, much  more than I'd realised!!           ":0)!X!
Have a wonderful week,& best wishes!!  ..XxX...

author: @yannmassard3970
text: hello, made one with only 1 step down (on one side only) so the output goes in front, circling the down stepped rollers. The efficiency, backdrivability and ratios are incredible. But they are really hard to get perfect with print, added TPU covers on all rings and rollers to create traction. I use them on a small robot, and I m surprised they re not more popular. On the archimede s videos, I got in touch with them through comment section, and they helped me a lot getting it right. Peace

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they are very helpful folks
